reasoning: The Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal one. The informal claim "exactly (p-1)/2 of {1,...,p-1} are QRs and (p-1)/2 are QNRs" is equivalent to the existence of two Finsets S₁, S₂ ⊆ ZMod p \ {0} of size (p-1)/2 consisting entirely of squares and non-squares respectively: since the total number of nonzero elements is p-1 and (p-1)/2 + (p-1)/2 = p-1 (using p odd), having at least (p-1)/2 of each forces exactly (p-1)/2 of each. The hypothesis `hodd : p % 2 = 1` surfaces the implicit premise that p is an odd prime, which is needed for the statement to be true (e.g., for p=2 the claim fails), and per the rubric this is a faithful reading. The encoding `∃ x : ZMod p, x^2 = q` correctly captures "q is a quadratic residue", and excluding 0 matches {1,...,p-1}.
